description: A commemorative MLK Day graphic. It is a high-contrast, two-tone (deep blue and white) stylized stencil portrait of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.'s face filling most of the square frame, rendered in a graphic, posterized style reminiscent of screen-print / Shepard-Fairey 'HOPE'-era political art. The face is built from blue and white shapes with small black speckle detailing; King looks slightly off-camera. In the lower-left, white serif text reads 'MLK' in large letters with '1929-1968' beneath it (his birth and death years). The bottom-left corner carries the official seal of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security and the bold 'ICE' wordmark. The style is somber and reverent, using a single dominant color and clean typography. The messaging technique is institutional holiday observance / values-association: an immigration-enforcement agency aligning itself with the civil-rights legacy of Dr. King and an 'environment free of discrimination' (per the tweet), which research may note as ironic given the agency's mission. Target audience: general public and the agency's social-media followers on the MLK Day holiday.
notable_text: MLK / 1929-1968 / ICE / U.S. Department of Homeland Security
